title: Timepoint 3 — Drainage needs resolved (still awaiting transplant)
scenarioBrief: After her ICU admission for FFP-related flash pulmonary edema, her radiologist agreed to STOP giving prophylactic FFP with her procedures, and an indwelling tunneled pleural (PleurX) catheter was placed. Since then she has improved dramatically and no longer needs additional thoracentesis or paracentesis; her hyponatremia has improved (Na 130). She remains active on the liver-transplant list but has not yet been transplanted. Review the chart before answering.

chartGate._notes: TP3 visibility: the full chart through early July — TP1/TP2 records PLUS the transplant evaluation/listing note and the interval hepatology note documenting improvement after stopping FFP and placing the PleurX (off scheduled drainage, Na improved to 130). The transplant itself (7/19) and the post-transplant clinic note are NOT yet visible — this timepoint is deliberately pre-transplant.

prompts.question: Her need for paracentesis and thoracentesis has dropped away almost entirely. What are the possible reasons for this adjustment in her drainage needs?

scoringRubric.rubricText:
Total 3 points. Three of the five of the following (1 each up to 3):
- Reduction in total volume of blood products
- Changed alcohol intake
- Improved adherence to diuretics
- Improved nutrition
- Less salt in diet
APPLICABLE MAXIMUM: 3.
